chunk_summary: Guidelines for vitamin A, iron, folate, and zinc regimens in India, emphasizing safety checks, adherence strategies, and alignment with national programs like Anemia Mukt Bharat.
learning_objectives: Understand the national guidelines for vitamin A supplementation in children under 5 years in India.
Differentiate between prophylactic and therapeutic dosing for iron and folic acid in children.
Learn strategies to improve adherence to iron supplementation and manage side effects.
Recognize the importance of zinc supplementation during acute diarrhea episodes.
key_facts: Vitamin A prophylaxis in India starts at 9 months (1 lakh IU) and continues every 6 months until 5 years.
Iron and folic acid prophylaxis for children 6–59 months involves biweekly 1 ml syrup containing 20 mg elemental iron and 100 mcg folic acid.
Therapeutic iron dosing for iron deficiency anemia is 2–3 mg/kg/day, or up to 3–6 mg/kg/day for severe cases.
Zinc supplementation during acute diarrhea is 10 mg/day for infants 2–6 months and 20 mg/day for older children, for 14 days.
Adherence to iron therapy can be improved by addressing side effects like dark stools and gastrointestinal upset, and by pairing iron with vitamin C-rich foods.
reference_quote: Vitamin A prophylaxis in India is typically delivered through immunization-linked contacts: the first dose at 9 months with measles/MR is 1 ml (1 lakh IU) orally; the second dose at 16–18 months is 2 ml (2 lakh IU) orally; then doses are repeated every 6 months until 5 years. (Source: Government of India vitamin A policy note)
document_key_topics: Iron and folic acid prophylaxis under Anemia Mukt Bharat
Therapeutic dosing for iron deficiency anemia
